channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: Research Associate – Foreign Policy and Security Studies at CSEP (The Africa Research Alliance), New Delhi, India
item.dc:creator: Hope N. Magwira
item.description:
Exciting Research Career Opportunity in Foreign Policy and Security Studies Professionals and emerging researchers interested in international relations, foreign policy, security studies, and Africa-focused research now have an excellent opportunity to join one of India’s leading public policy think tanks. The Centre for Social and Economic Progress (CSEP) is currently hiring a Research Associate for […]

The post Research Associate – Foreign Policy and Security Studies at CSEP (The Africa Research Alliance), New Delhi, India appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Exciting Research Career Opportunity in Foreign Policy and Security Studies

Professionals and emerging researchers interested in international relations, foreign policy, security studies, and Africa-focused research now have an excellent opportunity to join one of India’s leading public policy think tanks. The Centre for Social and Economic Progress (CSEP) is currently hiring a Research Associate for The Africa Research Alliance (TARA), a development-oriented research hub operating within its Foreign Policy and Security Studies programme.

This position offers a unique chance to contribute to policy-relevant research, engage with experts and policymakers, and support initiatives that strengthen understanding of Africa’s development, governance, security, and international relations. The role is based in New Delhi, India, and is a full-time position.

About the Centre for Social and Economic Progress (CSEP)

The Centre for Social and Economic Progress (CSEP) is an independent public policy think tank dedicated to conducting rigorous research and analysis on critical issues affecting India and the world. The organization is known for producing evidence-based policy recommendations and fostering dialogue among policymakers, researchers, academics, and development practitioners.

CSEP’s research spans several thematic areas, including:

  • Economic growth and development
  • Finance and development
  • Energy and sustainability
  • Foreign policy and security
  • Governance and public policy
  • International cooperation and diplomacy

The institution has established itself as a respected voice in public policy research and continues to influence policy discussions through high-quality research and stakeholder engagement.

About The Africa Research Alliance (TARA)

The Africa Research Alliance (TARA) serves as a dedicated research platform within CSEP’s Foreign Policy and Security Studies vertical. The initiative focuses on Africa-related development and policy issues while promoting deeper understanding and engagement between India and African countries.

As geopolitical, economic, and security relationships between Africa and the rest of the world continue to evolve, TARA plays an important role in generating research that supports informed policy decisions and meaningful partnerships.

Role and Responsibilities

The selected Research Associate will play an important role in supporting the alliance’s research activities and policy engagement efforts. Key responsibilities are expected to include:

  1. Conducting policy and academic research on foreign policy, security, and development issues.
  2. Monitoring and analyzing emerging policy developments and geopolitical trends.
  3. Supporting the preparation of research papers, policy briefs, reports, and analytical publications.
  4. Assisting in stakeholder engagement activities and policy dialogues.
  5. Contributing to the organization of workshops, conferences, seminars, and research events.
  6. Supporting collaborative research initiatives involving experts, policymakers, and development practitioners.

The role provides an excellent opportunity for individuals seeking practical experience in policy research and international affairs.

Why You Should Apply

Working at CSEP offers several professional advantages, including:

1. Exposure to High-Level Policy Research

Employees contribute to research that addresses pressing regional and global challenges and informs public policy discussions.

2. Work with Leading Experts

Researchers collaborate with experienced scholars, policy specialists, and international experts across multiple disciplines.

3. Build a Strong Professional Network

The organization regularly engages with government officials, diplomats, academics, development organizations, and industry stakeholders.

4. Gain Africa-Focused Research Experience

The position provides valuable exposure to African development, diplomacy, governance, and security issues, making it especially attractive for candidates interested in Africa-related policy research.

5. Enhance Research and Analytical Skills

The role requires rigorous research, policy analysis, writing, and stakeholder engagement, helping candidates strengthen skills that are highly valued across academia, government, think tanks, and international organizations.

Location

  • New Delhi, India
  • Full-Time Position

Application Information

Interested candidates should review the complete vacancy announcement and application instructions through the official CSEP careers page.

Deadline

The job announcement currently available does not publicly specify a closing date. Interested applicants are encouraged to submit their applications as early as possible before the position is filled.

Final Thoughts

The Research Associate – Foreign Policy and Security Studies position at CSEP represents an outstanding opportunity for professionals seeking to build a career in policy research, international affairs, security studies, and Africa-focused development research. Through The Africa Research Alliance, successful candidates will have the chance to contribute to impactful research, engage with influential stakeholders, and help shape important policy conversations concerning Africa, India, and the broader international community.

For aspiring policy researchers, international relations graduates, and development professionals, this position offers a strong platform for career growth and meaningful impact in the world of public policy research.

Discover more global job opportunities on OFY here

The post Research Associate – Foreign Policy and Security Studies at CSEP (The Africa Research Alliance), New Delhi, India appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Nigeria Green Academy 2026: Fully Funded Leadership and Capacity-Building Programme for Young Nigerian Change-Makers
item.dc:creator: Hope N. Magwira
item.description:
Applications Open for the Nigeria Green Academy 2026 Young Nigerians who are passionate about driving social, political, economic, and environmental change now have an exciting opportunity to join one of the country’s most impactful leadership and capacity-building programmes. The Heinrich Böll Foundation Abuja Office has officially opened applications for the Nigeria Green Academy 2026, an […]

The post Nigeria Green Academy 2026: Fully Funded Leadership and Capacity-Building Programme for Young Nigerian Change-Makers appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications Open for the Nigeria Green Academy 2026

Young Nigerians who are passionate about driving social, political, economic, and environmental change now have an exciting opportunity to join one of the country’s most impactful leadership and capacity-building programmes. The Heinrich Böll Foundation Abuja Office has officially opened applications for the Nigeria Green Academy 2026, an annual initiative designed to empower emerging leaders with the knowledge, skills, networks, and tools needed to contribute meaningfully to Nigeria’s development. The application deadline is 17 June 2026.

The Nigeria Green Academy has become a highly respected platform for nurturing young leaders from diverse sectors and backgrounds. Through intensive learning experiences, networking opportunities, and leadership development activities, the programme helps participants strengthen their ability to influence positive change within their communities and across Nigeria.

About the Nigeria Green Academy

The Nigeria Green Academy is an annual programme established by the Heinrich Böll Foundation Abuja Office. It was created to provide young and aspiring civil society activists, journalists, academic scholars, artists, and social entrepreneurs with opportunities to expand their knowledge, improve their practical skills, and build valuable professional networks.

The academy focuses on helping participants address some of Nigeria’s most pressing governance, development, environmental, social, and political challenges. It serves as a platform where young leaders can exchange ideas, develop innovative solutions, and strengthen their commitment to creating a more sustainable and inclusive future for Nigeria.

Over the years, the programme has developed a growing community of young Nigerians who continue to engage in public service, advocacy, social entrepreneurship, policy engagement, environmental sustainability, and community development. The academy seeks to create lasting connections among participants and foster a network that supports collaboration, idea-sharing, and mutual growth.

Objectives of the Programme

The Nigeria Green Academy aims to:

  1. Develop leadership capacity among young Nigerians.
  2. Strengthen participants’ understanding of governance and development issues.
  3. Equip participants with practical advocacy and engagement skills.
  4. Promote innovative thinking and problem-solving.
  5. Build a nationwide network of emerging leaders.
  6. Encourage active citizenship and democratic participation.
  7. Foster dialogue on social justice, sustainability, and inclusive development.

What Participants Can Expect

The programme utilizes a variety of learning methods and interactive approaches designed to encourage critical thinking and active participation.

Participants will engage in:

  • Self-reflection exercises.
  • Advocacy and leadership development sessions.
  • Interactive discussions and dialogue forums.
  • Knowledge-sharing activities.
  • Networking opportunities with fellow participants.
  • Exposure to experienced practitioners and thought leaders.
  • Collaborative problem-solving exercises.
  • Vision-building sessions focused on Nigeria’s future.

These activities help participants broaden their perspectives while developing practical skills that can be applied within their professional and community work.

Who Is the Programme Looking For?

The Nigeria Green Academy 2026 seeks young individuals who have demonstrated commitment, passion, resilience, innovation, and determination in promoting societal and political change.

Applicants should have shown active engagement in issues related to:

  • Economic development.
  • Social justice.
  • Political participation.
  • Community transformation.
  • Environmental sustainability.
  • Governance and accountability.
  • Civic engagement.

The programme intentionally promotes diversity and inclusion by ensuring balanced representation across gender, geography, and identity groups. The organizers strongly encourage female applicants to apply.

Benefits of Participating in the Nigeria Green Academy

Successful applicants will gain access to numerous benefits, including:

1. Leadership Development

Participants receive training designed to strengthen leadership and change-making capabilities.

2. Expanded Professional Networks

The programme connects participants with fellow leaders, activists, researchers, journalists, and innovators from across Nigeria.

3. Enhanced Advocacy Skills

Participants develop practical skills for influencing policy, engaging stakeholders, and driving community action.

4. Access to Knowledge and Resources

The academy provides exposure to new ideas, tools, frameworks, and strategies relevant to governance and development challenges.

5. Long-Term Community Membership

Participants become part of a growing alumni network of young Nigerians committed to positive change.

How to Apply

Interested applicants should submit the following:

  • Full name.
  • Phone number.
  • Email address.
  • Short biography.
  • A brief statement of interest explaining why they wish to join the programme (maximum 250 words).

Applications should be sent to:

green.academy@ng.boell.org

Shortlisted candidates will be contacted and invited to provide additional information as part of the selection process.

Click here to learn more about this opportunity

Important Application Notes

  • Applicants must be willing to participate for the entire duration of the programme.
  • Multiple submissions from the same applicant are strongly discouraged.
  • Early application is recommended due to the competitive nature of the programme.

Application Deadline

The deadline for submitting applications for the Nigeria Green Academy 2026 is 17 June 2026. Interested candidates are encouraged to apply as soon as possible to avoid missing this opportunity.

Final Thoughts

The Nigeria Green Academy 2026 offers a remarkable opportunity for young Nigerians who want to strengthen their leadership abilities, expand their professional networks, and contribute meaningfully to national development. Whether you are an activist, journalist, scholar, artist, or social entrepreneur, the programme provides a valuable platform to learn, connect, and grow alongside other passionate change-makers.

For young leaders seeking to create lasting impact in Nigeria, the Nigeria Green Academy 2026 could be the stepping stone toward greater influence, stronger skills, and a broader vision for transformative change.

Discover more global fellowship opportunities on OFY here

The post Nigeria Green Academy 2026: Fully Funded Leadership and Capacity-Building Programme for Young Nigerian Change-Makers appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: USDA Announces $9.75 Million Grant Funding Opportunity to Support Maple Syrup Industry and Food Security Initiatives in 2026
item.dc:creator: Hope N. Magwira
item.description:
The United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) has announced more than $9.75 million in grant funding through two important programs designed to strengthen agricultural development, expand market opportunities, support food security, and promote sustainable food production. The funding is being made available through the Acer Access and Development Program (Acer) and the Micro-Grants for Food […]

The post USDA Announces $9.75 Million Grant Funding Opportunity to Support Maple Syrup Industry and Food Security Initiatives in 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) has announced more than $9.75 million in grant funding through two important programs designed to strengthen agricultural development, expand market opportunities, support food security, and promote sustainable food production. The funding is being made available through the Acer Access and Development Program (Acer) and the Micro-Grants for Food Security Program (MGFSP). These programs aim to support agricultural stakeholders, local communities, research institutions, tribal governments, and state agencies working to improve food systems and agricultural sustainability across eligible regions.

According to the USDA Agricultural Marketing Service (AMS), the combined funding will help promote the domestic maple syrup industry while also increasing the quantity and quality of locally produced food in communities experiencing food insecurity. This investment reflects USDA’s ongoing commitment to strengthening local food systems, supporting producers, and encouraging innovation within the agricultural sector.

Breakdown of Available Funding

The USDA has allocated funding across two separate grant programs:

1. Acer Access and Development Program (Acer)

The Acer Access and Development Program will receive $5 million in available funding. The program is specifically designed to support projects that strengthen and expand the domestic maple syrup industry in the United States.

2. Micro-Grants for Food Security Program (MGFSP)

The Micro-Grants for Food Security Program will receive $4.75 million in funding. The program focuses on improving food security in communities that face challenges related to food production and dependence on imported food supplies.

Together, these programs represent a significant investment in agricultural development and food resilience.

Understanding the Acer Access and Development Program

The Acer Access and Development Program is a competitive grant initiative established to promote and strengthen the maple syrup industry through education, research, sustainability initiatives, and market development efforts. The program is authorized under the 2018 Farm Bill and receives annual appropriations from Congress.

Key Objectives of the Acer Program

The program supports activities that:

  • Promote research related to maple syrup production.
  • Enhance education and training for maple syrup producers.
  • Encourage sustainable management of natural resources used in maple production.
  • Increase consumer awareness and understanding of maple syrup products.
  • Improve marketing opportunities for maple syrup and maple-sap products.
  • Encourage landowners to expand maple-sugaring activities.
  • Promote public access to land suitable for maple-sugaring activities.

Types of Projects Supported

The Acer program supports two primary project categories:

  1. Market Development and Promotion Projects
  2. Producer and Landowner Education Projects

Eligible projects can receive funding ranging from $200,000 to $500,000, with project periods extending up to 36 months. Applicants are required to provide a cost share contribution equal to 25% of the federal funding requested.

Understanding the Micro-Grants for Food Security Program (MGFSP)

The Micro-Grants for Food Security Program is designed to strengthen local food production and reduce food insecurity in regions that rely heavily on imported food. The program helps communities improve their ability to produce fresh food locally while supporting small-scale agricultural activities.

Areas Supported by MGFSP

Funding may support initiatives such as:

  • Small-scale gardening projects.
  • Community agriculture initiatives.
  • Herding operations.
  • Livestock production.
  • Community-based food security projects.
  • Local food production and distribution systems.

Through this program, eligible state and territorial agricultural agencies receive funding and subsequently provide subawards to organizations, groups, and individuals implementing local food security projects.

Why This Funding Matters

Food security and agricultural sustainability continue to be major priorities globally. By investing in local food production and strengthening agricultural industries such as maple syrup production, USDA aims to:

  • Increase agricultural resilience.
  • Support rural economic development.
  • Improve local food availability.
  • Strengthen community food systems.
  • Create new market opportunities for producers.
  • Encourage agricultural innovation.
  • Promote sustainable land management practices.

These investments are expected to create long-term benefits for producers, consumers, communities, and agricultural institutions.

Application Process

Applications must be submitted electronically here. USDA encourages applicants to carefully review all eligibility requirements, application guidelines, and funding priorities before submitting proposals. The agency also provides informational webinars, frequently asked questions, and grants management support to help applicants prepare competitive applications.

Applicants are advised to begin the application process early to ensure all requirements are met before the deadline.

Applicants can learn more about this opportunity here

Application Deadline

The application deadline for both the Acer Access and Development Program and the Micro-Grants for Food Security Program is:

22 June 2026 at 11:59 p.m. Eastern Time. Applications submitted after the deadline may not be considered unless exceptional circumstances can be documented.

Final Thoughts

The USDA’s announcement of more than $9.75 million in grant funding represents a valuable opportunity for agricultural stakeholders, research institutions, tribal governments, and state agencies to strengthen food systems and agricultural development. Whether through expanding the maple syrup industry or supporting community-based food security initiatives, these grants have the potential to generate lasting economic, environmental, and social benefits.

Organizations and agencies working in agriculture, food security, rural development, and sustainability should explore these funding opportunities and prepare strong applications before the June 22, 2026 deadline.

Discover more global grants opportunities on OFY here

The post USDA Announces $9.75 Million Grant Funding Opportunity to Support Maple Syrup Industry and Food Security Initiatives in 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Crop Trust Power of Diversity Grants 2026: Funding Opportunities for Projects Promoting Crop Diversity, Food Security, and Sustainable Agriculture
item.dc:creator: Hope N. Magwira
item.description:
Organizations, institutions, researchers, educators, community groups, and agricultural advocates working to promote crop diversity and sustainable food systems now have an exciting opportunity to access funding through the Crop Trust Power of Diversity Grants Programme. The initiative is designed to support innovative projects that raise awareness about the importance of crop diversity and its critical […]

The post Crop Trust Power of Diversity Grants 2026: Funding Opportunities for Projects Promoting Crop Diversity, Food Security, and Sustainable Agriculture appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Organizations, institutions, researchers, educators, community groups, and agricultural advocates working to promote crop diversity and sustainable food systems now have an exciting opportunity to access funding through the Crop Trust Power of Diversity Grants Programme. The initiative is designed to support innovative projects that raise awareness about the importance of crop diversity and its critical role in strengthening food security, nutrition, climate resilience, and sustainable agriculture worldwide.

Launched by the Crop Trust, the Power of Diversity Grants Programme seeks to inspire individuals and organizations to develop impactful projects that help communities better understand the value of conserving and utilizing crop diversity. Through small grants, the programme aims to support creative approaches that engage the public, promote education, encourage collaboration, and highlight how crop diversity contributes to healthier and more resilient food systems.

As global food systems face increasing challenges from climate change, biodiversity loss, environmental degradation, and growing food insecurity, initiatives that protect and promote agricultural biodiversity have become more important than ever. The Crop Trust’s grant programme provides a platform for organizations and individuals to contribute to this important mission while receiving financial support for their efforts.

About the Crop Trust

The Crop Trust is an international organization dedicated to conserving crop diversity and ensuring that the world’s crop genetic resources remain available for future generations. The organization works with gene banks, governments, research institutions, and agricultural partners around the world to protect plant genetic resources that are essential for global food security and agricultural sustainability.

Crop diversity plays a crucial role in ensuring that food systems remain resilient to changing environmental conditions. Diverse crop varieties help farmers adapt to pests, diseases, extreme weather events, and changing climate patterns while contributing to improved nutrition and food security.

Through initiatives such as the Power of Diversity campaign, the Crop Trust seeks to increase public awareness of these issues and encourage greater engagement in efforts to protect agricultural biodiversity.

Purpose of the Power of Diversity Grants Programme

The Power of Diversity Grants Programme was established to support activities that communicate the importance of crop diversity to broader audiences. The programme recognizes that public awareness and education are essential components of biodiversity conservation and sustainable agriculture.

The grants are intended to support projects that:

  • Increase public awareness of crop diversity.
  • Highlight the importance of conserving crop genetic resources.
  • Promote sustainable food systems.
  • Encourage discussions around food security and nutrition.
  • Demonstrate the connection between crop diversity and climate resilience.
  • Engage communities in agricultural biodiversity initiatives.
  • Support innovative educational and outreach activities.

The programme welcomes creative ideas that can effectively communicate these important themes to diverse audiences.

Funding Available

Selected projects will receive financial support to implement activities that align with the goals of the programme.

The grants are designed to support relatively small but impactful initiatives that can reach communities, educational institutions, farmers, policymakers, consumers, and other stakeholders interested in food systems and biodiversity.

Funding can help cover project implementation costs, outreach activities, awareness campaigns, educational materials, events, workshops, exhibitions, communication initiatives, and other related activities that contribute to the programme’s objectives.

Types of Projects Encouraged

The Crop Trust encourages a wide range of innovative proposals. Examples may include:

Educational Initiatives

  • School programmes.
  • University projects.
  • Educational campaigns.
  • Learning materials and toolkits.
  • Public awareness workshops.

Community Engagement Activities

  • Community exhibitions.
  • Agricultural fairs.
  • Awareness events.
  • Public dialogues.
  • Farmer engagement activities.

Media and Communication Projects

  • Documentary productions.
  • Podcasts.
  • Digital storytelling.
  • Social media campaigns.
  • Photography projects.
  • Multimedia awareness campaigns.

Creative and Cultural Activities

  • Art exhibitions.
  • Theatre performances.
  • Storytelling initiatives.
  • Cultural events promoting agricultural biodiversity.
  • Interactive public engagement projects.

Research Communication Projects

  • Public dissemination of research findings.
  • Science communication initiatives.
  • Knowledge-sharing platforms.
  • Outreach activities connecting researchers and communities.

The programme places strong emphasis on creativity, innovation, and public engagement.

Why Crop Diversity Matters

Crop diversity is one of the foundations of global food security. Diverse crops help ensure that communities have access to nutritious foods while enabling agricultural systems to adapt to environmental and economic challenges.

Benefits of crop diversity include:

1. Improved Food Security

A diverse agricultural system reduces dependence on a limited number of crops and strengthens resilience against crop failures.

2. Enhanced Nutrition

Different crops provide different nutrients, helping support healthier and more balanced diets.

3. Climate Change Adaptation

Crop diversity allows farmers to select varieties that can withstand drought, flooding, heat, and other climate-related challenges.

4. Biodiversity Conservation

Preserving crop genetic resources helps protect valuable traits that may be needed for future agricultural innovation.

5. Sustainable Agriculture

Diverse farming systems often contribute to healthier ecosystems and more sustainable food production practices.

Selection Criteria

Applications will generally be assessed based on:

  • Relevance to crop diversity and agricultural biodiversity.
  • Potential impact and reach.
  • Innovation and creativity.
  • Feasibility of implementation.
  • Quality of the proposed activities.
  • Ability to engage target audiences effectively.

Projects that demonstrate strong potential for public engagement and awareness creation are expected to be particularly competitive.

How to Apply

Interested applicants should carefully review the official grant guidelines and application requirements available through the Crop Trust website.

Applicants are encouraged to develop clear project plans that demonstrate how their activities will contribute to raising awareness about crop diversity and promoting sustainable food systems.

Strong applications should clearly outline:

  • Project objectives.
  • Target audience.
  • Planned activities.
  • Expected outcomes.
  • Proposed budget.
  • Timeline for implementation.

Application Timeline

The Crop Trust has provided the following timeline for the Power of Diversity Grants Programme:

  • Application Deadline: 3 July 2026
  • Notification of Successful Grantees: 10 August 2026
  • Project Kick-off: 1 October 2026
  • Project Completion Deadline: Before 31 March 2029

Applicants are encouraged to submit their proposals well before the application deadline to allow sufficient time for review and any necessary revisions. Successful grantees will be notified in August 2026 and will have approximately two months to prepare for project implementation before activities officially commence in October 2026. All funded projects must be completed and deliver their intended outcomes before 31 March 2029.

Discover more grants opportunities on OFY here

The post Crop Trust Power of Diversity Grants 2026: Funding Opportunities for Projects Promoting Crop Diversity, Food Security, and Sustainable Agriculture appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Project Coordinator Opportunity at Seefar: Join a Global Social Enterprise Driving Positive Change
item.dc:creator: Hope N. Magwira
item.description:
Applications Open for Project Coordinator Position at Seefar Professionals seeking a meaningful international career in project management, migration, development, research, communications, and social impact now have an exciting opportunity to join Seefar as a Project Coordinator. Seefar, a global social enterprise known for delivering innovative solutions to complex social challenges, is currently recruiting talented individuals […]

The post Project Coordinator Opportunity at Seefar: Join a Global Social Enterprise Driving Positive Change appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications Open for Project Coordinator Position at Seefar

Professionals seeking a meaningful international career in project management, migration, development, research, communications, and social impact now have an exciting opportunity to join Seefar as a Project Coordinator. Seefar, a global social enterprise known for delivering innovative solutions to complex social challenges, is currently recruiting talented individuals who are passionate about creating positive change across communities worldwide.

This role offers a unique opportunity to contribute to impactful projects that address migration, displacement, economic development, social inclusion, community resilience, and information access. Successful candidates will work within an international team while supporting projects that influence policy, improve lives, and generate sustainable outcomes in some of the world’s most challenging environments.

For professionals interested in combining project management skills with social impact, this position provides valuable experience within a globally recognized organization that operates across Africa, Asia, Europe, and the Middle East.

About Seefar

Seefar is a purpose-driven social enterprise that specializes in designing and delivering solutions to complex social, economic, and migration-related challenges. The organization works with governments, international organizations, donors, NGOs, and communities to improve access to information, create economic opportunities, strengthen resilience, and support vulnerable populations.

Through evidence-based approaches and innovative programme delivery models, Seefar helps individuals make informed decisions while supporting governments and development partners in achieving long-term social impact.

The organization operates in numerous countries and has built a strong reputation for delivering projects that are practical, measurable, and sustainable.

About the Project Coordinator Role

The Project Coordinator position is designed for highly organized, motivated, and detail-oriented professionals who can support the successful implementation of multiple projects across different regions and thematic areas.

The successful candidate will play a critical role in ensuring projects are delivered efficiently, on time, and in accordance with donor requirements and organizational standards.

This position requires strong coordination, communication, planning, and administrative skills, making it ideal for professionals who enjoy managing complex tasks while working with diverse international teams.

Key Responsibilities

As a Project Coordinator, responsibilities may include:

1. Project Management Support

  • Assist in planning and coordinating project activities.
  • Monitor project implementation and progress.
  • Support project reporting and documentation.
  • Ensure activities remain aligned with project objectives.
  • Track deliverables and milestones.

2. Administrative Coordination

  • Organize meetings and project-related events.
  • Maintain project records and documentation.
  • Coordinate schedules and timelines.
  • Support procurement and logistical activities when required.

3. Stakeholder Engagement

  • Communicate with project partners and stakeholders.
  • Support collaboration with donors and implementing partners.
  • Facilitate coordination between internal and external teams.

4. Monitoring and Reporting

  • Collect project information and data.
  • Support preparation of donor reports.
  • Assist in monitoring project outcomes and performance indicators.
  • Maintain accurate project records and reporting systems.

5. Financial and Operational Support

  • Assist with budget monitoring.
  • Track project expenditures.
  • Support financial reporting processes.
  • Help ensure compliance with project and donor requirements.

Why Consider a Career at Seefar?

Working at Seefar offers numerous professional and personal benefits.

International Exposure

Employees collaborate with professionals and stakeholders from different countries and sectors, providing valuable global experience.

Meaningful Impact

Projects directly contribute to improving lives, strengthening communities, supporting migrants, and promoting sustainable development.

Professional Development

The organization provides opportunities to strengthen skills in project management, communications, stakeholder engagement, research, and programme implementation.

Diverse Working Environment

Team members work in multicultural environments with colleagues from various professional and cultural backgrounds.

Career Growth Opportunities

The role offers exposure to complex international development programmes, creating opportunities for long-term professional advancement.

Ideal Candidate Profile

Seefar is seeking candidates who are proactive, adaptable, and capable of managing multiple priorities simultaneously.

Successful applicants typically demonstrate:

  • Strong organizational skills.
  • Excellent communication abilities.
  • Attention to detail.
  • Problem-solving capabilities.
  • Time management skills.
  • Ability to work independently and collaboratively.
  • Experience supporting projects and programme implementation.
  • Commitment to social impact and development objectives.

Skills and Experience

Candidates are expected to possess relevant professional experience and educational qualifications that enable them to contribute effectively to project delivery.

Preferred competencies include:

  • Project coordination experience.
  • Administrative and operational support experience.
  • Stakeholder engagement skills.
  • Report writing and documentation abilities.
  • Budget tracking and financial monitoring experience.
  • Strong Microsoft Office and digital collaboration skills.
  • Experience working in international development, migration, research, communications, consulting, or related sectors.

Benefits of the Position

The Project Coordinator role offers a range of professional advantages:

  • Opportunity to work on international projects.
  • Exposure to global development and migration issues.
  • Collaboration with leading experts and practitioners.
  • Development of project management competencies.
  • Strengthening of communication and coordination skills.
  • Participation in impactful social initiatives.
  • Flexible and international working environment.

Application Process

Interested candidates should review the complete job description and submit their here

Applicants are encouraged to carefully tailor their applications to demonstrate relevant project coordination experience, stakeholder management skills, administrative capabilities, and commitment to social impact work.

Strong applications should clearly highlight previous achievements, project management experience, and examples of successfully coordinating activities across multiple stakeholders.

Click here to learn more about this Job opportunity

Application Deadline

Applications for the Project Coordinator position are being reviewed on a rolling basis. Interested candidates are encouraged to apply as early as possible, as the vacancy may close once a suitable candidate has been identified.

Final Thoughts

The Project Coordinator position at Seefar represents an outstanding opportunity for professionals seeking a career that combines project management with meaningful social impact. Through its work across migration, development, resilience, and information access initiatives, Seefar continues to deliver innovative solutions that improve lives and strengthen communities around the world.

For individuals with strong coordination skills, a passion for international development, and a desire to contribute to positive change, this role offers valuable experience, professional growth, and the opportunity to be part of a mission-driven global organization.

Discover more global job opportunities on OFY here

The post Project Coordinator Opportunity at Seefar: Join a Global Social Enterprise Driving Positive Change appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: ARUA Early-Career Research Fellowship 2026–2027: Six-Month Research Opportunities Across Africa
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
The African Research Universities Alliance (ARUA), with support from the Mastercard Foundation and the Carnegie Corporation of New York, has opened applications for the next round of its Early-Career Research Fellowship Programme. This initiative will support up to 42 emerging African researchers to conduct cutting-edge, collaborative research at ARUA’s Centres of Excellence (CoEs) and Clusters […]

The post ARUA Early-Career Research Fellowship 2026–2027: Six-Month Research Opportunities Across Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The African Research Universities Alliance (ARUA), with support from the Mastercard Foundation and the Carnegie Corporation of New York, has opened applications for the next round of its Early-Career Research Fellowship Programme. This initiative will support up to 42 emerging African researchers to conduct cutting-edge, collaborative research at ARUA’s Centres of Excellence (CoEs) and Clusters of Research Excellence (CoREs).

This announcement focuses only on the second application cycle for the upcoming fellowship year.

Fellowship Duration and Key Dates

The programme offers six-month residential fellowships to be undertaken at designated ARUA CoEs or CoREs across the continent.

For the second fellowship period, applicants may apply for:

  • Fellowship Period: September 2026 – February 2027
  • Application Deadline: 15 July 2026

This upcoming round prioritises high-potential researchers who can commit to full-time residency at the host institution and contribute meaningfully to existing or emerging research streams.

To promote equity in the African research landscape, at least 70% of all fellowships are reserved for women, and applicants must be 35 years or younger at the time of application.

About ARUA

The African Research Universities Alliance brings together leading universities across Africa with a shared commitment to strengthening high-quality research and graduate training. ARUA’s approach is built on collaboration—pooling resources, expertise, and infrastructure to support African-led research that addresses the continent’s socio-economic challenges.

Across its 13 Centres of Excellence, ARUA supports pioneering work in both the natural and social sciences, spanning fields such as:

  • Climate change and development
  • Sustainable food systems
  • Water and energy
  • Migration, mobility, governance, and inequality
  • Urbanisation and post-conflict reconstruction

Complementing this network are 22 Africa–Europe Clusters of Research Excellence, jointly established with The Guild of European Research Universities, aligned with AU–EU Innovation Agenda priorities.

About the Mastercard Foundation

The Mastercard Foundation is one of the world’s largest philanthropic organisations, dedicated to expanding access to quality education and financial inclusion. Through long-term partnerships across Africa, the Foundation supports young people in securing dignified and fulfilling work. It operates independently of the Mastercard company, with offices across Africa and Canada.

About the Carnegie Corporation of New York

Founded by Andrew Carnegie, the Carnegie Corporation of New York is one of the oldest grantmaking foundations in the United States. Its work focuses on education, democratic resilience, international peace and security, and strengthening higher education and research in Africa.

Research Environment: ARUA CoEs and CoREs

The fellowship provides access to ARUA’s network of 13 Centres of Excellence, which include thematic areas such as:

  • Water
  • Energy
  • Non-Communicable Diseases
  • Sustainable Food Systems
  • Migration and Mobility
  • Good Governance
  • Urbanisation and Habitable Cities

In addition, candidates may be hosted by one of 22 Clusters of Research Excellence, structured under four themes:

Public Health

  • Infectious diseases, multimorbidity, preparedness and response, genomics, health and gender

Green Transition

  • Nature-based solutions, renewable energy, water resource management, conservation biology

Innovation and Technology

  • AI and data science, creative economies, engineering for the future

Capacities for Sciences

  • Peace research, inequalities, sustainable development politics

Roles and Responsibilities

Selected fellows will be expected to:

  • Conduct collaborative research under mentor guidance
  • Publish at least one high-impact journal article (first-author or co-authored)
  • Deliver two research presentations and participate in technical workshops
  • Support the development of new research partnerships
  • Assist in disseminating research findings through institutional platforms

Eligibility Requirements

Applicants must:

  • Be African nationals with a PhD completed within the last five years
  • Be under 35 years old
  • Hold an academic or research position at an African university (non-ARUA institutions may apply)
  • Demonstrate strong research output and alignment with a specific CoE or CoRE
  • Be able to spend six months in residence at the host institution
  • Have existing data or research material relevant to their proposed project

Women candidates are strongly encouraged to apply, given the 70% reserved allocation.

Contract and Remuneration

The fellowship provides:

  • US$2,000 monthly stipend
  • Accommodation support
  • Return travel to the host institution
  • Eligibility-based financial support for women researchers requiring adjustment assistance
  • Office space at the host CoE or CoRE

No relocation expenses are included.

Application Components

Applicants must submit:

  • Cover letter detailing academic background, motivation, preferred CoE/CoRE, and residency plan
  • Full CV with personal details, research output, and referee information
  • Letter of Support from home institution confirming return
  • Proof of PhD award
  • A concept note (max. 1,500 words) with a feasible six-month research plan
  • Two samples of academic writing (not from the doctoral thesis)
  • Passport biodata page

Applications must be submitted through ARUA’s official online portal.

Applications can be accessed here.

To discover more global Fellowship opportunities, click here.

The post ARUA Early-Career Research Fellowship 2026–2027: Six-Month Research Opportunities Across Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: University of Alberta PhD in Political Science 2026: A Comprehensive Doctoral Program for Future Scholars, Researchers, and Policy Leaders
item.dc:creator: Diakaridia KONE
item.description:
The PhD Program in Political Science at the University of Alberta offers an intensive and intellectually stimulating pathway for students who wish to pursue advanced research and make meaningful contributions to political knowledge and public affairs. Designed for highly motivated scholars, the program provides extensive training in political science theory, research methodologies, specialized fields of […]

The post University of Alberta PhD in Political Science 2026: A Comprehensive Doctoral Program for Future Scholars, Researchers, and Policy Leaders appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The PhD Program in Political Science at the University of Alberta offers an intensive and intellectually stimulating pathway for students who wish to pursue advanced research and make meaningful contributions to political knowledge and public affairs. Designed for highly motivated scholars, the program provides extensive training in political science theory, research methodologies, specialized fields of study, and professional development.

Located within the Department of Political Science at the University of Alberta in Edmonton, Canada, this doctoral program is intended for students who are committed to conducting original research and developing expertise in a specific area of political science. The degree typically requires a minimum of four years of study and research, although students have up to six years to complete all degree requirements.

The program emphasizes academic excellence, independent scholarship, and career preparation both within and beyond academia. Graduates are equipped to pursue careers in higher education, government, public policy, international organizations, non-profit institutions, think tanks, research agencies, and private-sector organizations.

Why Choose the University of Alberta PhD in Political Science?

Pursuing a doctoral degree is a significant commitment that requires dedication, intellectual curiosity, and a passion for research. The University of Alberta recognizes this challenge and provides doctoral students with comprehensive academic support throughout their journey.

The department encourages prospective applicants to carefully consider their career goals and evaluate how a doctoral degree aligns with their professional ambitions. At the same time, the program actively supports students pursuing careers both inside and outside traditional academic pathways.

Key strengths of the program include:

  • Rigorous doctoral-level training.
  • Opportunities for advanced independent research.
  • Multiple specialization options.
  • Comprehensive examination preparation.
  • Flexible dissertation formats.
  • Strong faculty supervision.
  • Professional development opportunities.
  • Career preparation for academic and non-academic sectors.
  • Access to a vibrant research community.

Program Duration

The PhD in Political Science is a demanding research degree that generally requires:

  • Minimum completion time: 4 years.
  • Maximum completion time: 6 years.
  • Mandatory residency requirement: 2 academic years of full-time attendance at the University of Alberta.

Students spend much of their doctoral journey conducting self-directed research focused on a specialized political science topic.

Academic Requirements

The doctoral curriculum requires students to complete:

  • 18 graduate-level credits.
  • Typically equivalent to six 3-credit graduate courses.

Required Course

All doctoral students must complete:

  • POLS 680.

This core course is offered annually, usually during the Fall semester.

Students must also participate in:

  • Graduate Lab training.

The Graduate Lab is specifically designed to prepare students for their Comprehensive Examinations.

Areas of Specialization

Each PhD student must select two areas of specialization from the following fields:

1. Canadian Politics

Students explore:

  • Canadian political institutions.
  • Public policy.
  • Federalism.
  • Political behavior.
  • Governance and democratic processes.

2. Comparative Politics

Focus areas may include:

  • Comparative political systems.
  • Democratization.
  • Political development.
  • State-building.
  • Comparative public policy.

3. Gender and Politics

Students examine:

  • Gendered political structures.
  • Feminist political theory.
  • Representation and participation.
  • Public policy and gender equality.

4. International Relations

Research may focus on:

  • Global governance.
  • International organizations.
  • Security studies.
  • Diplomacy.
  • International political economy.

5. Political Theory

Students investigate:

  • Classical political thought.
  • Contemporary political philosophy.
  • Democratic theory.
  • Justice and ethics.

Additional coursework is normally selected from 500-level and 600-level Political Science courses offered by the University of Alberta.

Year-by-Year Program Structure

Year 1

Students complete:

  • Required coursework.
  • Specialized training.
  • Graduate Lab participation.
  • Initial supervisory arrangements.

By the end of the first year, students officially designate a supervisor or nominate a new supervisor if appropriate.

Year 2

Doctoral students complete:

  • Comprehensive Examination in Specialization Area One.
  • Comprehensive Examination in Specialization Area Two.

These examinations evaluate students’ mastery of their chosen fields and readiness for advanced research.

Year 3

After successfully completing comprehensive examinations, students proceed to:

  • Dissertation proposal development.
  • Oral candidacy examination.

The candidacy examination evaluates the quality and feasibility of the proposed dissertation project.

Before the end of Year 3, students must complete:

  • Comprehensive examinations.
  • Candidacy examination.
  • Language requirement.
  • Ethics training requirement.
  • All remaining program requirements except the dissertation.

Once these milestones are achieved, students become official PhD Candidates, often referred to as:

ABD (All But Dissertation).

Years 3–6

Students focus primarily on:

  • Conducting original research.
  • Writing the dissertation.
  • Publishing scholarly work.
  • Presenting research findings.
  • Preparing for career opportunities after graduation.

Dissertation Options

The University of Alberta offers two dissertation pathways.

Option 1: Traditional Dissertation

The traditional doctoral dissertation generally contains:

  • 50,000–80,000 words.
  • 4–7 integrated chapters.
  • Introduction.
  • Conclusion.
  • Bibliography.
  • Appendices and research data where applicable.

The dissertation must:

  • Present original research.
  • Demonstrate rigorous analysis.
  • Contribute substantially to knowledge.
  • Meet publication-quality scholarly standards.

Dissertation Examination Process

When the supervisory committee approves the dissertation:

  1. The dissertation is sent to two independent examiners.
  2. One examiner must be an external expert from outside the University of Alberta.
  3. All five examiners participate in the final oral defense.

Option 2: Dissertation-by-Publication

Students may alternatively choose a dissertation-by-publication format.

This format includes:

  • An introduction chapter.
  • Multiple journal-style research papers.
  • A conclusion chapter.

The major difference between the two formats lies in the body of the dissertation:

  • Traditional format = integrated monograph chapters.
  • Publication format = independent publishable journal articles.

Students are encouraged to discuss dissertation format options with their supervisor early in the program.

The dissertation format must be approved before the candidacy examination.

Language Requirement

Every doctoral student must demonstrate proficiency in a language other than English.

This requirement can be fulfilled through:

Option 1: Previous Coursework

Completion of:

  • A second-year undergraduate language course.
  • Within the previous five years.

Option 2: Translation Examination

Students may complete:

  • Translation of a 400–500-word scholarly text into coherent and grammatically correct English.

Language selection may relate to:

  • The geographical area of research.
  • The political traditions under investigation.
  • Relevant historical or contemporary political literature.

Students specializing in Canadian Politics are typically expected to demonstrate proficiency in French.

Students whose native language is not English may, under certain circumstances, satisfy this requirement automatically if additional language competency is not required for their dissertation research.

Ethics Training Requirement

All graduate students at the University of Alberta must complete:

Graduate Ethics Training (GET)

Total required training:

  • 8 hours.

Requirements include:

  • 5 hours completed through the Faculty of Graduate and Postdoctoral Studies.
  • 3 additional hours through:
    • Department seminars on academic integrity.
    • Approved university workshops and seminars.

Professional Development Requirement

Students entering graduate programs at the University of Alberta are required to complete a professional development component.

This requirement is designed to strengthen:

  • Research skills.
  • Leadership abilities.
  • Career readiness.
  • Communication competencies.
  • Professional networking.

Supervision and Faculty Support

Admission to the PhD program is closely connected to faculty supervision.

Students are admitted when:

  • Their research interests align with a faculty member’s expertise.
  • A faculty member agrees to supervise their proposed project.

This supervisory relationship plays a crucial role in:

  • Research design.
  • Dissertation development.
  • Publication planning.
  • Academic mentoring.
  • Career preparation.

Students work closely with:

  • A primary supervisor.
  • Two additional faculty members serving on the supervisory committee.

Career Opportunities After Graduation

Graduates of the PhD in Political Science may pursue careers in:

Academia

  • University professor.
  • Lecturer.
  • Research fellow.
  • Academic administrator.

Government and Public Policy

  • Policy analyst.
  • Government advisor.
  • Legislative researcher.
  • Public administration specialist.

International Organizations

  • Political affairs officer.
  • Development specialist.
  • Governance consultant.
  • International policy advisor.

Non-Profit Sector

  • Advocacy specialist.
  • Program director.
  • Research manager.
  • Public policy consultant.

Private Sector

  • Political risk analyst.
  • Corporate affairs advisor.
  • Strategic consultant.
  • Research specialist.

Contact Information

Graduate Advisor

Department of Political Science
University of Alberta

Email: psgrad@ualberta.ca

Department Email:
poliscea@ualberta.ca

Phone:
(780) 492-3429

Address:
Department of Political Science
10-16 Henry Marshall Tory Building
University of Alberta
Edmonton, Alberta, Canada T6G 2H4

Deadline

No specific application deadline was provided in the available program information. Applicants should consult the University of Alberta Graduate Studies admissions pages and contact the Graduate Advisor for the latest intake dates, admission cycles, and application requirements.

The University of Alberta PhD in Political Science offers an outstanding opportunity for ambitious researchers to develop advanced expertise, contribute original scholarship, and prepare for impactful careers across academia, government, international organizations, and the broader public policy landscape.

 

Apply here

 

Learn More

  • For more information about this opportunity, Click here 
  • More global PhD opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here

The post University of Alberta PhD in Political Science 2026: A Comprehensive Doctoral Program for Future Scholars, Researchers, and Policy Leaders appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Global Good Fund Fellowship 2027 for Social Entrepreneurs Worldwide: Leadership Development Fellowship for High-Impact Changemakers
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
Applications are now open for the Global Good Fund Fellowship 2027, one of the world’s leading leadership development fellowships designed specifically for social entrepreneurs who are creating measurable impact in their communities and are ready to scale both their leadership and their organizations. The Global Good Fund Fellowship is a prestigious international programme that supports […]

The post Global Good Fund Fellowship 2027 for Social Entrepreneurs Worldwide: Leadership Development Fellowship for High-Impact Changemakers appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for the Global Good Fund Fellowship 2027, one of the world’s leading leadership development fellowships designed specifically for social entrepreneurs who are creating measurable impact in their communities and are ready to scale both their leadership and their organizations.

The Global Good Fund Fellowship is a prestigious international programme that supports visionary social entrepreneurs who are addressing some of society’s most pressing challenges through innovative and sustainable enterprises. The fellowship focuses on leadership transformation, recognizing that the growth of a social enterprise is directly connected to the growth of its founder or leader.

Entrepreneurs from around the world who are working to create positive social change in areas such as education, health, environmental sustainability, economic mobility, and financial inclusion are encouraged to apply for this highly competitive opportunity.

Unlike many entrepreneurship programmes that focus solely on business growth, the Global Good Fund Fellowship places leadership development at the center of the experience. Fellows receive personalized support, mentorship, executive coaching, and access to a powerful global network that helps them become more effective leaders while expanding the impact of their enterprises.

About the Global Good Fund

The Global Good Fund is an international organization dedicated to accelerating the leadership development of social entrepreneurs who are tackling critical social and environmental challenges.

The organization believes that strong leaders create stronger organizations and greater social impact. Through its fellowship programmes and leadership initiatives, the Global Good Fund invests in individuals who are driving transformative change across communities and countries.

Over the years, the fellowship has supported entrepreneurs from diverse sectors and regions, helping them strengthen their leadership capabilities and increase their ability to create sustainable impact.

About the Global Good Fund Fellowship 2027

The Global Good Fund Fellowship is specifically designed for social entrepreneurs who have already demonstrated traction and impact through their ventures and are now seeking to strengthen their leadership capacity.

The fellowship supports entrepreneurs at a critical stage of growth where leadership development can significantly influence organizational success and long-term sustainability.

The programme is intended for leaders who:

  • Are committed full-time to their enterprises
  • Have demonstrated measurable social impact
  • Lead organizations with growth potential
  • Have decision-making authority within their enterprises
  • Are ready to scale their leadership alongside their organizations

The fellowship creates opportunities for participants to reflect on their leadership journey, identify growth areas, and develop strategies that enhance both personal effectiveness and organizational impact.

Why Leadership Development Matters

Many social entrepreneurs possess innovative ideas and strong commitment to social change. However, scaling impact often requires leadership skills that extend beyond technical expertise.

The Global Good Fund Fellowship addresses this challenge by helping participants:

  • Build self-awareness
  • Strengthen decision-making abilities
  • Improve communication skills
  • Enhance team leadership capacity
  • Increase resilience and adaptability
  • Develop strategic thinking capabilities
  • Expand influence and stakeholder engagement

By investing in the entrepreneur, the fellowship helps strengthen the entire enterprise.

Fellowship Benefits

Selected fellows gain access to a range of valuable opportunities and resources.

1. Personalized Leadership Development

Participants receive tailored support to identify and address their unique leadership challenges and opportunities.

2. Executive Coaching

The programme provides access to experienced executive coaches who help fellows refine their leadership approach and achieve professional goals.

3. Mentorship Opportunities

Fellows are connected with mentors who offer strategic guidance, industry insights, and practical support.

4. Global Network Access

Participants join an international community of social entrepreneurs, mentors, experts, investors, and changemakers.

5. Strategic Growth Support

The programme helps entrepreneurs position their organizations for sustainable growth and long-term impact.

6. Increased Visibility

Fellows gain recognition and exposure through association with one of the world’s leading social entrepreneurship networks.

7. Long-Term Professional Development

The fellowship equips entrepreneurs with leadership tools and frameworks that continue to benefit them long after the programme ends.

Focus Areas and Sectors

The Global Good Fund particularly encourages applications from enterprises working in:

Financial Inclusion

Initiatives that improve access to financial services and economic opportunities for underserved populations.

Environment

Projects that promote sustainability, climate action, environmental protection, and responsible resource management.

Education

Innovative solutions that expand access to quality education and lifelong learning opportunities.

Economic Mobility

Enterprises creating pathways to employment, entrepreneurship, and economic advancement.

Health

Organizations addressing healthcare access, public health challenges, and wellbeing.

Importantly, applicants working outside these sectors are also welcome if they demonstrate significant social impact and potential for systemic change.

Eligible Enterprise Types

The Global Good Fund recognizes that social innovation can take many forms.

Eligible enterprises may include:

For-Profit Organizations

Businesses generating revenue while delivering measurable social impact.

Non-Profit Organizations

Mission-driven organizations focused on addressing social challenges.

Hybrid Models

Organizations combining commercial and social approaches to create sustainable impact.

Eligibility Criteria

Applicants must meet the following requirements:

Enterprise Requirements

  • Enterprise must be at least two years old for the flagship fellowship programme
  • Enterprise must have at least one full-time employee in addition to the applicant
  • Enterprise should demonstrate an effective business model or strong potential for long-term scalability

Applicant Requirements

  • Must be committed full-time to the enterprise
  • Must hold primary decision-making authority
  • Must be founder or key organizational leader
  • Must demonstrate leadership potential
  • Must be actively creating social impact

Early-Stage Entrepreneurs

The Global Good Fund also welcomes applications from leaders whose enterprises are less than two years old.

While such applicants may not qualify for the flagship fellowship programme, their applications may be considered for other opportunities within the Global Good Fund ecosystem.

Selection Process

The fellowship selection process consists of three phases.

Phase 1: Initial Application and Eligibility Screening

Dates:
June 1 – June 30, 2026

During this stage, applications are reviewed to determine eligibility and programme fit.

Phase 2: Detailed Questionnaire

Dates:
July 10 – August 11, 2026

Selected applicants will complete a more comprehensive questionnaire about themselves and their enterprises.

Phase 3: Virtual Interviews

Dates:
September 8 – October 24, 2026

Shortlisted candidates participate in interviews with the selection team.

Final Selection

Selected fellows will be notified by:

November 30, 2026

What Makes a Strong Application?

Successful applicants typically demonstrate:

  • Strong leadership potential
  • Clear social impact
  • Sustainable business model
  • Commitment to growth
  • Ability to scale impact
  • Deep understanding of the problem they are solving
  • Strong vision for the future

Applicants should clearly articulate both organizational achievements and personal leadership goals.

Why You Should Apply

The Global Good Fund Fellowship offers much more than traditional entrepreneurship support. It provides a transformative leadership journey designed to help social entrepreneurs maximize their effectiveness and expand their impact.

For leaders who are committed to solving social and environmental challenges while growing themselves as changemakers, this fellowship represents a unique opportunity to access world-class mentorship, coaching, leadership development, and a global network of fellow innovators.

If you are building a mission-driven organization and are ready to take your leadership to the next level, the Global Good Fund Fellowship 2027 could be the opportunity that accelerates your journey toward lasting impact.

Click HERE to learn more and apply!

Application Deadline

July 1, 2026

Interested social entrepreneurs are encouraged to apply early and carefully prepare their submissions.

Discover more global Fellowship and Leadership Development opportunities on OFY!

The post Global Good Fund Fellowship 2027 for Social Entrepreneurs Worldwide: Leadership Development Fellowship for High-Impact Changemakers appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: MATS Research Fellowship 2026: Fully Funded AI Alignment & Security Program in Berkeley or London (Stipend, Housing, Mentorship & $20K Compute Included)
item.dc:creator: Phionah Tumusiime
item.description:
The MATS Research fellowship is a highly competitive, fully funded research and training program designed to prepare the next generation of researchers working on AI alignment, AI security, and frontier AI risk reduction. Hosted in major research hubs such as Berkeley and London, the program provides participants with financial support, mentorship from world-leading AI researchers, […]

The post MATS Research Fellowship 2026: Fully Funded AI Alignment & Security Program in Berkeley or London (Stipend, Housing, Mentorship & $20K Compute Included) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The MATS Research fellowship is a highly competitive, fully funded research and training program designed to prepare the next generation of researchers working on AI alignment, AI security, and frontier AI risk reduction. Hosted in major research hubs such as Berkeley and London, the program provides participants with financial support, mentorship from world-leading AI researchers, and access to substantial compute resources.

This fellowship is designed for individuals who want to contribute meaningfully to ensuring advanced artificial intelligence systems are safe, transparent, and beneficial to humanity.

Program Overview

The MATS Research fellowship is structured as an intensive 10 to 12-week in-person research program that brings together talented fellows and mentors from top AI institutions. Participants work directly with experts from organizations such as:

  • Anthropic
  • OpenAI
  • Google DeepMind
  • Redwood Research
  • ARC and other frontier AI safety groups

The program is designed to provide a deeply immersive environment where fellows can focus entirely on research without financial or logistical barriers.

Key Program Details

  • Location: Berkeley (USA) or London (UK)
  • Duration: 10–12 weeks
  • Start Date: September 28, 2026
  • Application Deadline: June 7, 2026
  • Funding Package Includes:
    • $12,500 stipend
    • $20,000 compute budget
    • Free housing
    • Meals provided
    • Travel costs covered
    • J-1 visa support (if required)

Research Tracks Available

Fellows can apply to one or multiple research tracks depending on their interests and skills:

  1. Empirical Research
  2. Theoretical Research
  3. Strategy and Forecasting
  4. Policy and Governance
  5. Systems Security
  6. Field-building and Founding
  7. Biosecurity

Each track is designed to address different dimensions of AI safety, from technical alignment research to governance and societal impact analysis.

Mentorship and Research Environment

One of the strongest aspects of the MATS Research is its mentorship model. Fellows are paired with experienced researchers from leading AI labs and safety organizations.

Mentors provide:

  • Direct research guidance
  • Project direction and feedback
  • Technical and conceptual mentorship
  • Support in publishing and scaling research ideas

This mentorship-driven structure allows fellows to rapidly grow their research skills while contributing to real-world AI safety work.

Fellowship Benefits

The program provides a comprehensive support system so fellows can focus fully on research:

Financial and Material Support

  • $12,500 stipend
  • $20,000 compute allocation
  • Free accommodation
  • Free meals
  • Travel reimbursement

Academic and Professional Growth

  • Collaboration with top AI researchers
  • Access to cutting-edge AI safety problems
  • Opportunities to publish research papers
  • Exposure to frontier AI labs and institutions

Community and Networking

  • Cohort-based learning environment
  • Strong global AI safety community
  • Networking with researchers and organizations in AI governance and technical alignment

Application Process

The application process is multi-stage and designed to evaluate both technical ability and research potential.

Stage 1: General Application

  • Open until June 7, 2026
  • Takes 1–2 hours
  • Includes background questions and track-specific responses
  • Requires contact details for two references

Stage 2: Central Evaluation

Applicants who pass Stage 1 proceed to standardized assessments:

  • Empirical Track: research taste and technical implementation tests
  • Policy & Governance Track: analytical writing evaluation
  • Other tracks: may proceed directly to Stage 3

Stage 3: Stream Applications

  • Applicants rank specific research streams
  • Submit tailored responses or research proposals
  • May include interviews or work tests

Final Offers

  • Decisions finalized in late July / early August 2026
  • Matching process considers both mentor and applicant preferences
  • Program begins September 28, 2026

Impact and Outcomes

The MATS Research has built a strong track record of impact in the AI safety ecosystem:

  • 80% of alumni work in AI alignment, transparency, or security fields
  • 190+ research publications in recent years
  • Over 12,000 citations across research outputs
  • Strong presence in frontier AI organizations and academic institutions

Why This Fellowship Matters

As AI systems become increasingly powerful, the need for robust safety research grows rapidly. This program is designed to:

  • Develop skilled researchers in AI alignment
  • Support early-career talent in high-impact research
  • Strengthen global AI safety infrastructure
  • Bridge academia, industry, and policy communities

Participants often go on to work at leading AI labs or contribute to influential safety research initiatives.

Official Application Link

Learn more and apply here

Conclusion

The MATS Research fellowship is a rare opportunity for aspiring researchers to engage directly with frontier AI safety work in a highly supported environment. With full funding, mentorship from top institutions, and access to significant compute resources, it is one of the most impactful programs for those interested in shaping the future of safe artificial intelligence.

Click here for more global Fellowship opportunities

 

The post MATS Research Fellowship 2026: Fully Funded AI Alignment & Security Program in Berkeley or London (Stipend, Housing, Mentorship & $20K Compute Included) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: European Central Bank Research Analyst Jobs 2026 in Germany: Earn €4,982 Monthly Plus Benefits
item.dc:creator: Phionah Tumusiime
item.description:
The European Central Bank (ECB) is now accepting applications for multiple Research Analyst positions within its Economic Analysis and Policy divisions. This is an exceptional opportunity for talented graduates and early-career professionals who are passionate about economics, finance, public policy, financial stability, international relations, and research. Based in Frankfurt am Main, Germany, successful candidates will […]

The post European Central Bank Research Analyst Jobs 2026 in Germany: Earn €4,982 Monthly Plus Benefits appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The European Central Bank (ECB) is now accepting applications for multiple Research Analyst positions within its Economic Analysis and Policy divisions. This is an exceptional opportunity for talented graduates and early-career professionals who are passionate about economics, finance, public policy, financial stability, international relations, and research.

Based in Frankfurt am Main, Germany, successful candidates will join one of the world’s most influential financial institutions and contribute directly to the research, analysis, and policy work that supports the European Central Bank’s decision-making processes.

With a competitive monthly net salary of €4,982 plus benefits, opportunities for career growth, and the possibility of obtaining a permanent contract after three years, this opportunity offers an outstanding career path for aspiring economists and policy professionals.

About the European Central Bank

The European Central Bank is the central bank responsible for managing the euro, the official currency used by 21 European Union countries.

The ECB’s primary objective is to maintain price stability across the euro area and protect the purchasing power of the euro. To achieve this, the institution conducts economic research, monitors financial markets, evaluates monetary conditions, and develops policies that support sustainable economic growth and financial stability.

The ECB employs professionals from across Europe and offers a highly international, multicultural, and intellectually stimulating work environment.

Research Analyst Positions Available

The ECB is recruiting Research Analysts across several departments, including:

  • Economics
  • International and European Relations
  • Macroprudential Policy and Financial Stability
  • Monetary Policy
  • Research

Successful applicants will work alongside economists, researchers, analysts, and policy experts to support economic and financial analysis that informs ECB policy decisions.

What Will Research Analysts Do?

Research Analysts play a critical role in supporting evidence-based policymaking at the ECB.

Key responsibilities include:

Economic and Financial Analysis

  • Monitor economic developments across the euro area.
  • Analyze financial market trends.
  • Support forecasting exercises.
  • Assess economic conditions in both advanced and emerging economies.

Data Collection and Management

  • Collect and manage large datasets.
  • Develop data infrastructures.
  • Process structured and unstructured data.
  • Ensure data quality and consistency.

Econometric and Quantitative Research

Research Analysts will:

  • Develop econometric models.
  • Build analytical tools.
  • Support research projects.
  • Conduct statistical and quantitative analysis.
  • Contribute to evidence-based policy recommendations.

Machine Learning and Advanced Analytics

Depending on the department, analysts may:

  • Work with machine learning techniques.
  • Utilize deep learning methods.
  • Analyze large-scale datasets.
  • Develop innovative approaches to economic and financial research.

Policy Support

Analysts will contribute to:

  • Internal policy briefings.
  • Research notes.
  • Technical reports.
  • Official ECB publications.
  • Presentations supporting policy discussions.

Benefits of Working at the ECB

Successful candidates will receive a highly attractive package.

Financial Benefits

  • Monthly net salary of €4,982
  • Additional employment benefits
  • Stable public institution employment

Career Development

The ECB actively invests in employee development through:

  • Professional training programs
  • Mentoring opportunities
  • Cross-departmental mobility
  • Exposure to senior policymakers and experts

International Work Environment

Employees benefit from:

  • Multicultural teams
  • International collaboration
  • Exposure to global economic issues
  • Networking opportunities across Europe

Permanent Career Opportunities

One of the strongest benefits of this recruitment cycle is that some contracts may become permanent after three years, depending on:

  • Individual performance
  • Organizational needs
  • Budget considerations

Part-Time and Flexible Working Options

The ECB is also offering flexible arrangements.

Some positions are available on a 50% part-time basis, making them particularly suitable for candidates who are:

  • Pursuing a PhD
  • Completing postgraduate studies
  • Engaged in academic research alongside employment

Eligibility Requirements

Applicants must meet the following requirements.

Educational Requirements

Candidates must possess:

  • A Bachelor’s degree or equivalent in:
    • Economics
    • Finance
    • Econometrics
    • Data Science
    • Mathematics
    • Political Science
    • Or another relevant discipline

Experience Requirements

Applicants must have:

  • At least two years of practical experience

This may include experience gained through:

  • Internships
  • Research assistant positions
  • Work placements
  • Traineeships
  • Professional employment

Additionally, candidates must have at least 12 months of experience in:

  • Economic analysis
  • Economic policy
  • Research institutions
  • Central banks
  • International policy organizations

Technical Skills

Applicants should have advanced experience using:

  • Python
  • R
  • MATLAB
  • Stata

They should also possess experience in:

  • Data management
  • Economic modelling
  • Financial modelling
  • Econometric analysis

Language Requirements

Candidates must demonstrate:

  • Advanced English proficiency (C1 level)
  • Intermediate proficiency (B1 level) in another official EU language

Preferred Qualifications

While not mandatory, the ECB prefers candidates who have:

  • A Master’s degree
  • Knowledge of machine learning techniques
  • Understanding of deep learning applications
  • Experience with advanced quantitative research

Recruitment Process

The selection process will be conducted remotely and includes:

  1. Written assessment
  2. Presentation exercise
  3. Interview

Interview sessions are expected to take place between:

6–10 July 2026

Candidates who are not selected but perform well may be placed on an ECB reserve list and considered for future opportunities.

Why You Should Apply

Working at the European Central Bank offers a rare opportunity to contribute to policies that affect millions of people across Europe.

Research Analysts gain exposure to cutting-edge economic research, collaborate with leading experts, and develop skills that are highly valued in government institutions, international organizations, financial institutions, and academia.

For graduates and early-career professionals seeking a prestigious international career in economics, finance, policy, or research, this opportunity represents an outstanding pathway to professional growth.

Application Deadline

Deadline: 16 June 2026

Interested candidates should submit their applications before the closing date to be considered for these highly competitive positions.

Click here for more and to apply

Click here for more global job opportunities
